name: snyk
description: Query Snyk organizations, projects, and security issues

envVars.name: SNYK_TOKEN
envVars.description: Snyk API token (personal token or service account token)

tools.name: List Organizations
tools.description: List Snyk organizations the token can access

tools.name: List Projects
tools.description: List projects in a Snyk organization

names_start_with.description: Filter projects whose name starts with this string

historicalSummary.omitted: JSON:API relationship objects and project metadata

tools.name: Get Project
tools.description: Get a project's details in a Snyk organization

tools.name: List Issues
tools.description: List security issues in a Snyk organization, optionally filtered by severity or status

effective_severity_level.description: Comma-separated severities to include: low, medium, high, critical

status.description: Issue status filter: open or resolved

tools.name: Get Organization
tools.description: Get details of a Snyk organization

prompts.title: Critical vulnerabilities
prompts.prompt: List the open critical and high severity issues across my Snyk organization and summarize them by project.

prompts.title: Project overview
prompts.prompt: List my Snyk projects and show which ones were tested most recently.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Snyk API Setup

steps.title: Sign up for Snyk
steps.description: Create a free account at https://app.snyk.io and import at least one repository so projects and issues exist to query.

steps.title: Get your API token
steps.description: Go to Account Settings (https://app.snyk.io/account) and copy your personal API token, or create a service account token for shared use. Free plans include API access via the personal token.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add the token to your .env as SNYK_TOKEN=...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Organizations, then List Projects with one of the returned org IDs.

notes: Authentication uses 'Authorization: token <SNYK_TOKEN>' — the prefix is 'token', not 'Bearer'
Every REST API request requires a date-based ?version= query parameter; tools default to 2024-10-15
If your account is in a regional environment (e.g. US-02 at api.us.snyk.io), API hosts differ — these tools target the default https://api.snyk.io
Responses follow the JSON:API format: items are under data[] with attributes, pagination under links
